items.title: Further $105m federal-state bailout announced for Nyrstar smelters

children.content: The federal, Tasmanian and South Australian governments have contributed to a new funding package for Nyrstar's Hobart and Port Pirie smelters, including money for feasibility studies into modernising and expanding their operations.

items.title: Beekeepers call for access to new biopesticide to target varroa mite

children.content: A new biopesticide could help battle growing resistance to varroa mite treatments in Australia.

image.altText: a man in beekeeping outfit stares at testing tube

items.title: Mother says family's wait for maintenance on overcrowded rental 'breaks us'

children.content: Delays in maintenance repairs, including mould removal, are forcing families to sleep on mattresses in living rooms, adding to overcrowding pressures in remote homes.

image.altText: A woman standing in a kitchen holding a baby.

items.title: Seized greyhounds looked like 'RSPCA cases', kennel owner says

children.content: A dog kennel owner is searching for answers after Greyhound Racing SA put two dogs that appeared malnourished and one with an open wound "you could stick your finger into" in her care.
Warning: This article contains images and content that some readers may find distressing.

gallery.altText: A woman in a pink sweatshirt patting a greyhound.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a pink sweatshirt patting a greyhound.

items.title: Wanted man jailed for running over police officer during 'escape' attempt

children.content: A man, who was on the run from authorities in Victoria, has been jailed for eight years after mowing down a police officer in South Australia.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit and a police officer in full uniform leave a building with glass doors.

items.title: Officers 'acted appropriately' during arrest death at Clare, top cop says

children.content: SA Police Commissioner Grant Stevens says a preliminary view of the body worn camera vision from an incident at Clare on the weekend where a man died during an arrest shows officers "acted appropriately".

image.altText: multiple officers on the street

items.title: Family's 'worst nightmare' after purchasing home filled with defects

children.content: After a young family invests their life savings in their dream home, only to discover it is filled with defects, SA's consumer watchdog issues a public warning notice about the builder responsible and seven other traders.

image.altText: A woman and a man holding a young child in front of a house

items.title: Acting SA electoral commissioner calls for council election delay

children.content: South Australia's acting electoral commissioner says she wants council elections delayed until mid-2027 while it awaits a review into the drama-plagued state and Voice elections earlier in the year.

image.altText: A sign for Town of Walkerville with a 66 on top of a verandah

items.title: 'When Neale spoke, people felt it': Daniher farewelled at MCG funeral

children.content: Family, friends, members of the AFL community and dignitaries are attending the service to remember the AFL legend, MND campaigner and former Australia of the Year.

gallery.altText: A dark haired man with a beard leans over and kisses a coffin draped in the Australian flag with native flowers on top.
The crowd in the MCG stands at Neale Daniher's funeral wears Big Freeze beanies
A painting of a man between the Australian and Aboriginal flag above a coffin draped in the Australian flag and native flowers.

items.title: The Big Issue celebrates three decades in Australia with special edition

children.content: The magazine has become a lifeline for its vendors and is marking its 30th year with a special edition, pouring more proceeds in the pockets of those who need it most.

image.altText: A group of people wearing fluorescent vests hold 30th anniversary posters with celebratory moods.

items.title: Mickey Mouse and friends freeze Super Netball finals out of prime venue

children.content: The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new standard with a record crowd at one of the biggest stadiums in the country. But in 2026, the sport has been forced to retreat to smaller finals venues due to a scheduling issue with Disney on Ice.

image.altText: Lights shine in the crowd with a netball hoop in the foreground

items.title: SA government says crackdown is leading to illicit tobacco price increase

children.content: South Australia's Consumer and Business Affairs Minister says a price increase for illegal tobacco noticed by inspectors within stores shows the trade is being disrupted.

image.altText: Cigarettes and vapes on a table

items.title: SA farmers celebrate dramatic turnaround after record rain

children.content: Twelve months after drought left paddocks bare, record rainfall in parts of South Australia has left some farmers smiling at significant improvements.

image.altText: A man kneels amongst a green crop on a farm.

items.title: With his pet chihuahua and trusty gopher, 97yo grazier won't slow down

children.content: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

gallery.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him

thumbnail.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him

items.title: Highland cows encounters bring 'positive impact', agritourism operators say

children.content: Traipsing around a farm in the wake of a highland cow or donkey might seem like a quaint rural pastime, but those in the business of agritourism believe there are other benefits.

image.altText: Two large shaggy cows in a green paddock. One is looking directly atthe camera, the other is grazing the grass.

items.title: 'It's an unprecedented matter': Bondi gunman hit with 19 more charges

children.content: Akram is facing charges of murder and committing a terrorist act over the attack at Bondi Beach on 14 December.

image.altText: Naveed Akram

items.title: Protests erupt in Belfast after stabbing attack, homes, cars and bus set on fire

children.content: Protesters hold anti-immigration demonstrations in Northern Ireland and England after a Sudanese man is arrested over a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

image.altText: Car torched in street.

gallery.altText: Four people stand watching a bus on fire, in a Belfast high street. A fire crew attends.

thumbnail.altText: Four people stand watching a bus on fire, in a Belfast high street. A fire crew attends.

items.title: Too poor to leave Australia. These people are trapped with no safety net

children.content: A man dies. His body is not noticed for nearly a week. He was invisible to many, like the growing number of homeless Australia wide.

image.altText: A homeless person lays on a makeshift bed as commuters pass him by.

items.title: Thirteen government agencies embroiled in potential data breach

children.content: Thirteen government agencies are now embroiled in an expanding scandal involving potential data breaches prompting calls for an urgent audit amid concerns over national security.

image.altText: A stack of documents sitting in an office, generic.

items.title: Bear attack in Japan car park captured on CCTV as cities record more and more wild encounters

children.content: Bear encounters are rising in Japan, and the problem is not confined to the wilderness.

gallery.altText: A black-furred bear walks by a chain-link fence near a building.

thumbnail.altText: A black-furred bear walks by a chain-link fence near a building.

items.title: Why Xi's visit to North Korea is a flex for a new world order

children.content: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

image.altText: Two men wearing suits seen sitting and smiling in the crowd at a stadium.

items.title: ASX flat as US launches strikes on Iran, Australian EV sales continue to surge — as it happened

children.content: Supermarket giant Woolworths will begin consulting with segments of its 10,000 corporate workers today, with plans to offshore hundreds of roles to Asia. The Australian share market has begun its day relatively flat.

image.altText: The ASX building exterior.

items.title: Finding and saving the world's biggest trees before they disappear

children.content: It took a decade for researchers to find the tallest tree in East Asia. Now they know where the 84.1-metre conifer is, but the future of its species and other tall trees is dire.

image.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

items.title: Calling all budding storytellers living with disability

children.content: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

image.altText: two women stand alongside one another smiling. One of the women has her right arm amputated. They stand in an ABC studio.

items.title: Hayley's husband escalated control during her pregnancy. Her story is not uncommon

children.content: Pregnancy is a time of greater autonomy and self-awareness for women. Because domestic violence is about power and control, a pregnancy can challenge that for a person who is using abuse.

gallery.altText: illustration of woman holding her pregnant belly new version
illustration of woman holding her baby in bed

thumbnail.altText: illustration of woman holding her pregnant belly new version
illustration of woman holding her baby in bed

items.title: 'A pile of crap': Premier's mea culpa over Ogilvie scandal

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff says he "dropped the ball" regarding the allegations against a former minister, likening it to a "pile of crap" he has to clean up from "time to time".

image.altText: a man in a blue shirt and navy jacket and grey hair stands in front of plans

items.title: Musk's xAI hit with class action over power plant's 'inescapable' noise

children.content: US residents say a plant powering data centres has caused them emotional distress and reduced the value of their properties.

image.altText: a fenced industiral site - metal pies and tubes criss cross in front of a warehouse

items.title: The 35-year old Blues record Brian To'o can break in Origin II

children.content: Brian To'o is one of the best New South Wales Origin players in recent years and now history beckons for the Blues winger in Game II at the MCG.

image.altText: A man poses in a State of Origin jersey

items.title: How police may have inadvertently saved this man from Ivan Milat

children.content: Etched in Steven Clark's memory are the protruding teeth and moustache of the driver he says picked him up as a teenager — a man he now believes was serial killer Ivan Milat.

image.altText: Man wearing a hat stands on the side of a road.

items.title: Why was Rolf Harris never tried for his crimes in Australia?

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: Mining set to restart at one of the NT's biggest gold reserves

children.content: After being dormant for nearly 30 years, the Mount Todd gold mine near Katherine is gearing up to restart production. While there are calls for local Aboriginal people to have the "first crack" at mining jobs, the operator is planning to engage a largely FIFO workforce.

image.altText: A miner inspects a large rock.

items.title: Court rejects appeal to increase sentence of killer driver

children.content: The Court of Appeal has dismissed an appeal to increase the jail term of Christopher Dillon Joannidis, who killed five people in a horrific 2023 crash in Northern Victoria.

image.altText: Christopher Dillon Joannidis holding his partner Eleanor Theebom's hand walking across a road

items.title: Man found dead in Melbourne park had suffered burns, police say

children.content: Police say the man appeared to have suffered burns and was discovered by a passer-by in parkland in Melbourne's east.

image.altText: A police vehicle.

items.title: Shop worker 'concerned' by security response after knife comment

children.content: A retail worker at Sydney's Broadway Shopping Centre says she was left scared after a man told her he had a knife.

image.altText: Shivanya in grey top holding bag outside shopping centre

items.title: Is Australia sucking the joy out of childhood?

children.content: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: playtime.

gallery.altText: Two young white kids holding hands, running through long grass
